description: Animations and premium feel enhancements. Install vaul for native-feel bottom sheets. Add whileTap micro-interactions to primary buttons. Implement optimistic UI for cart actions. Standardize animation durations to 150-250ms. Integrate Vaul-based cart/product detail bottom sheet. Add staggered list animations. Optional: integrate Lenis for smooth scroll.
steps: Install vaul package: run npm install vaul. This is needed for native drag-to-dismiss bottom sheets.

Replace the custom BottomSheet in components/ui/bottom-sheet.tsx with a Vaul-based implementation. Vaul provides Drawer.Root, Drawer.Trigger, Drawer.Content, etc. Maintain the same API (open, onClose, title, children) for backward compatibility. Keep the drag-to-dismiss behavior. Use spring animation for the sheet.

In components/product-card.tsx - the add-to-cart button already has whileTap={{ scale: 0.85 }} on grid layout and whileTap={{ scale: 0.9 }} on horizontal layout. The quantity stepper buttons have whileTap={{ scale: 1.3/1.4 }}. Standardize: primary action buttons should use whileTap={{ scale: 0.96 }} as specified. Update the add button to scale: 0.96 and stepper increment/decrement to scale: 0.9.
In components/mobile-bottom-nav.tsx - the NavTab icons already have whileTap={{ scale: 0.85 }}. Update to scale: 0.92 for consistency with a premium feel (not too aggressive).
Add whileTap={{ scale: 0.96 }} to the checkout button in components/cart/cart-page-client.tsx. Wrap the Link in a motion.div or convert the Link to include motion props.

Implement optimistic UI for cart actions in components/cart/cart-provider.tsx. The cart is already client-side localStorage only (no DB sync for cart). The 'optimistic' pattern is already in place since cart state updates instantly in state and syncs to localStorage. Document this in a comment. If there IS a server sync needed (for logged-in users), add a background sync pattern with rollback. Currently cart is purely client-side - no server calls needed.

Standardize animation durations: Review all transition durations across components. The globals.css defines --motion-fast: 150ms and --motion-standard: 240ms. Ensure framer-motion transitions use durations in the 0.15-0.25s range. In components/home/animated-product-section.tsx, the stagger delays are 0.05-0.06s (fast) - these are stagger intervals, not durations, so fine. Spring physics with stiffness 200-400 and damping 20-30 produce ~200ms settle times. This is already correct.

Add spring easing configuration: Create a shared animation config in lib/motion.ts with presets like { type: 'spring', stiffness: 300, damping: 30 } that can be imported across components. Use this in new animations rather than defining inline.

Optional: Install lenis (npm install lenis) for smooth scroll. Create a LenisProvider component in components/lenis-provider.tsx and add it to providers.tsx. Apply it to the main scroll container. Skip if the existing scroll behavior is already smooth (CSS scroll-behavior: smooth and -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch are already applied).
acceptance_criteria: vaul package is installed and the BottomSheet component uses Vaul's Drawer API
All primary buttons (add to cart, checkout, quantity steppers) have whileTap micro-interactions with scale: 0.96 for primary, scale: 0.9 for secondary
A shared motion config (lib/motion.ts) provides reusable spring presets
Animation durations are standardized in the 150-250ms range (spring settle time)
No TypeScript or lint errors introduced
verification: Run npx next lint to confirm no lint errors
Run npx tsc --noEmit to confirm no type errors
